title: Chapter 1 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Flutter basics!

questions.question: What is Flutter?
questions.explanation: Flutter is a UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications.

questions.question: Flutter uses the Dart programming language.
questions.explanation: Yes, Flutter apps are written in Dart.

questions.question: The entry point of a Flutter app is the _____ function.
questions.explanation: Every Dart program starts with the main() function.

questions.question: Which command checks your Flutter installation?
questions.explanation: flutter doctor provides a health check.

questions.question: Hot reload resets the app state.
questions.explanation: Hot reload preserves state; hot restart resets it.

questions.question: Which folder contains your Dart source code?
questions.explanation: The lib/ folder is where you write your Flutter code.

questions.question: To create a new Flutter project, you use `flutter _____`.
questions.explanation: flutter create <project_name>

questions.question: pubspec.yaml is used to manage dependencies.
questions.explanation: Yes, it lists dependencies, assets, and fonts.

questions.question: What does the command `flutter run` do?
questions.explanation: flutter run compiles and runs the app on a connected device/emulator.

questions.question: You can use print() statements to debug in Flutter.
questions.explanation: Print output appears in the console.

questions.question: Which key combination triggers hot reload in VS Code?
questions.explanation: Saving the file (Ctrl+S) triggers hot reload in VS Code with Flutter extension.

questions.question: The main widget that wraps your app is usually called _____.
questions.explanation: Most Flutter apps use MaterialApp as the root widget.

questions.question: Flutter apps can only run on Android and iOS.
questions.explanation: Flutter also supports web, Windows, macOS, and Linux.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `flutter doctor --android-licenses` command?
questions.explanation: It accepts the required Android SDK licenses.

questions.question: To add an image asset, you declare it in the _____ section of pubspec.yaml.
questions.explanation: Assets are listed under the flutter: section, specifically flutter: assets:

questions.question: Hot reload works for changes to native code (Android/iOS).
questions.explanation: Hot reload only applies to Dart code changes.

questions.question: Which file contains the app's name and version?
questions.explanation: pubspec.yaml holds metadata like name, description, and version.

questions.question: The command to upgrade Flutter SDK is `flutter _____`.
questions.explanation: flutter upgrade updates to the latest stable Flutter.

questions.question: You need Xcode to build Flutter apps for iOS.
questions.explanation: Xcode is required for iOS development on macOS.

questions.question: What does the `flutter clean` command do?
questions.explanation: flutter clean removes the build/ directory, forcing a clean rebuild.

questions.question: The default starter app increments a _____ when you press a button.
questions.explanation: It's the classic Flutter counter app.

questions.question: You can run Flutter apps on a physical device without enabling developer mode.
questions.explanation: Developer options and USB debugging must be enabled on Android; iOS requires a developer account.

questions.question: Which command lists all connected devices?
questions.explanation: flutter devices shows connected physical and virtual devices.

questions.question: To use a Cupertino-style widget, you need to import `package:flutter/_____ .dart`.
questions.explanation: Cupertino widgets are in the cupertino library.

questions.question: Flutter's engine is written in C++.
questions.explanation: The Flutter engine (Skia, Dart runtime) is primarily C++.
